mission.title: backstage: Provide some default values + secrets for the Scaffolder context
mission.description: Provide some default values + secrets for the Scaffolder context. Requested by 22+ users.

steps.title: Check current backstage setup
steps.description:
Verify your backstage version and configuration:
```bash
npx backstage-cli info
```
This feature requires a working backstage installation.

steps.title: Review backstage configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant backstage configuration:
Following on from the closed #6632, it would be great if we could define some default values that can be used in the template manifest (`template.yaml`) and also in `fetch:template`.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Provide some default values + secrets for the Scaffolder…
steps.description:
As an aside here also, and maybe this changes the design a little bit, what if we were able to set default values that you could also use as default values in the `parameters` block. Maybe we don't want to store the default values on the `parameters` namespace then, as it's gonna get super
```yaml
# app-config.yaml 
...
scaffolder:
  defaultEnvironment:
    parameters:
      region: eu-west-1
    secrets:
      AWS_ACCESS_KEY: ${AWS_ACCESS_KEY}
...
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Provide some default values + secrets for the Scaffolder…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: As an aside here also, and maybe this changes the design a little bit, what if we were able to set default values that you could also use as default values in the `parameters` block.
codeSnippets:
# app-config.yaml 
...
scaffolder:
  defaultEnvironment:
    parameters:
      region: eu-west-1
    secrets:
      AWS_ACCESS_KEY: ${AWS_ACCESS_KEY}
...

apiVersion: scaffolder.backstage.io/v1beta3
kind: Template
metadata:
  name: v1beta3-demo
  title: Test Action template
  description: scaffolder v1beta3 template demo
spec:
  owner: backstage/techdocs-core
  type: service

  parameters:
    ...

    - title: Choose a location
      properties:
        region:
          title: Region
          type: string
    ...

  steps:
    ...

    - id: template
      name: Create Repo
      action: fetch:template
      input:
        region:  ${{ parameters.region }}
    - id: push
      name: Push to S3
      action: publish:s3
      input:
        region:  ${{ parameters.region }}
        accessToken: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_TOKEN }}
    ...


As an aside here also, and maybe this changes the design a little bit, what if we were able to set default

prerequisites.description: A working backstage installation or development environment.
